fix_proposals.description: Add argparse to web/server.py with --host, --port, --help flags
fix_proposals.detail: Add `import argparse` and `parser.add_argument('--port', type=int, default=8791)` and `parser.add_argument('--host', default='localhost')` at the bottom of the file. This makes `python3 web/server.py --help` work and enables test isolation via --port 0.

agent_analysis_request.instruction: Read the evidence_summary above. Apply the persona's framework. Add your analysis to report.agent_analysis_response with: top_risks (list), bugs_found (list), fix_proposals (list of {description, severity, file_path (if known), priority}).
agent_analysis_request.model: agent-native (no API call — agent uses own model)

agent_analysis_response.analyst: Claude Code (acting as Kent Beck persona)
agent_analysis_response.testability_assessment: web/server.py has ZERO argparse/CLI interface. It immediately tries to bind to a port when run as `python3 web/server.py`. This makes it untestable via CLI: you cannot call `--help`, `--port`, `--version` without first having a free port. This is a testability defect (Kent Beck: 'untestable code is broken code').
agent_analysis_response.what_to_test_first: 1. Does `python3 web/server.py --help` exit 0 with usage text? (currently: NO — exits 1 with OSError) 2. Does `python3 web/server.py --port 0` bind to ephemeral port? (for test isolation) 3. Does the server start cleanly on a fresh port? I fear: the server has no way to start with a test-safe configuration.
top_risks: No argparse: cannot pass --help, --port, --host without modifying code
Port hardcoded: OSError if port 8791 already in use (no fallback, no --port flag)
No graceful startup error: crash dumps instead of clean error message

bugs_found.description: web/server.py has no argparse — `--help` flag not handled, immediately tries to bind
bugs_found.evidence: exit_code=1, stderr contains OSError: Address already in use
bugs_found.heuristic: Kent Beck TDD: untestable CLI = broken CLI
